description: Let's have a horizontal scroll to scroll between questions and then also that should include an auto scroll once we finish working on we finish all the answers for a particular question. Also we should add an answer button somewhere where the user can draft an answer while on a particular question.

results.identified_issue: Three-part request. Part 3 (draft-an-answer button) is self-contained and shipped. Parts 1-2 (horizontal scroll between questions + auto-advance to the next question) are a real restructure of the heavily-tuned single-question vertical feed in DiscoverView, and they fork on a product decision — whether horizontal swiping bypasses the listen-lock. I did NOT unilaterally decide that for an app whose entire identity is the listen-gate; I implemented the unambiguous part and laid out the plan + the one question. Note: auto-advance to the next question already exists today, just vertically (the last page of a question's feed is the next question's card, and finishing the last clip auto-advances onto it via advanceToNextPage → select). The clean horizontal design extracts the per-question vertical feed into a QuestionFeedView and wraps the ordered question list in a horizontal pager (TabView .page), turning that handoff from vertical into horizontal. Needs on-device iteration once the interaction is confirmed.
results.summary: Added an Answer button to Discover; horizontal question-scroll planned pending one product call
results.detailed_description:
I split your three asks and shipped the one that was unambiguous, plus a concrete plan for the bigger two.

DONE — the Answer button. There's now a compose button (the pencil-in-square icon) in the Discover toolbar. Tap it and a composer opens already set to the question you're currently listening to — no more scrolling a picker to find it. Opening it pauses whatever's playing so you're not writing over an argument in your ear, you can type or dictate on-device as before, and saving drops the answer straight into the deck for that question and returns you to the feed. There's a Cancel button if you change your mind.

PLANNED — horizontal scroll between questions + auto-scroll (asks 1 and 2). These are a genuine restructure of the Discover feed, which today is a single VERTICAL scroll: question card → each answer → (last page) the next question's card. So 'auto-scroll to the next question when you finish all the answers' actually already happens — it's just vertical: finishing the last answer rolls you onto the next question automatically. Your ask is to make the between-questions axis HORIZONTAL: swipe left/right to change question, up/down to move through that question's answers, and when a question's answers run out it auto-slides sideways to the next one. The clean way to build it: pull the per-question feed into its own view and wrap the ordered question list in a horizontal pager, so each side-swipe is a new question and the vertical listen-machinery (the lock, the hands-free auto-advance) stays exactly as tuned inside each one.

Before I rewrite that tuned feed, one product call I don't want to make for you: should swiping sideways between questions be FREE (browse to any question anytime), or should it stay GATED like the vertical feed — you can slide back to questions you've already heard, but you can only move FORWARD to the next question by finishing the current one (or via auto-advance)? The whole app is built around 'you must hear both sides,' so free horizontal swiping could quietly become the skip button the vertical lock exists to prevent. Tell me which you want and I'll build it that way. (My lean: gated forward, free backward — it preserves the listen discipline while making 'move on' feel like a deliberate sideways step.)
